core_rule: If a problem is known but not fixed, record it.
core_principle: A known problem that is not recorded becomes future confusion.

problems.title: Allowed-To-Know Constraint Not Implemented
related_ledgers: North Star NS-04 + Wholesome Enrichment principle
problems.blocked_by: implementation (schema-design close-criterion now met by SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md §4 — landed 2026-04-29)
problems.problem: The system does not yet have a formal layer that separates: facts the client actually shared / facts from transcripts/comms / enrichment-only internal strategy / protected/off-limits facts / unverified facts / approval-required facts and actions.
problems.evidence: Brenda & Steve highlighted the risk: enrichment-derived context was useful internally but could leak into customer-facing copy. Jake explicitly said the project needs a 'what we're allowed to know' constraint. Wholesome Enrichment principle (NORTH_STAR.md §6) names the four-bucket model but no schema or workflow implements it.
problems.impact: Without this layer, automation may surface creepy or unsupported personalization, misuse enrichment, or treat internal strategy as customer-facing truth. Violates NS-04 and Wholesome Enrichment principle.
problems.current_workaround: During Client Box audits, manually flatten outbound copy and only use facts traceable to comms/transcripts or explicit approval. Brenda's plan carries this discipline now.
problems.recommended_next_action: Design Client Box-level allowed_to_use schema and workflow. Possible files: allowed_to_use.md, allowed_to_use.json, protected_facts.md, approval_required.md. Pair with Source-of-Truth Ledger when it lands.
close_criteria: schema exists ✅ (SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md §4 — 2026-04-29)
at least one Client Box uses it
scheduled-fire / read protocol checks it before composing
inbox guardrails reference it
Wholesome Enrichment principle is mechanically enforced not just documented

history.note: created — initial problem statement carried forward from Ledger 05 spec

history.event: partial. Source-of-Truth Ledger §4 codified four-bucket allowed-to-know schema + per-Box implementation contract. Status open→partial. Blocker shifted from schema-design to implementation. First close-criterion satisfied.

problems.title: Active Client Boxes Need Guardrail Audit
problems.owner: Jake + agent workflow (one box at a time)
problems.problem: Active Client Box plans may have been written before current guardrails were fully enforced. They may contain stale dates, risky commitment language, enrichment leakage, or send assumptions that no longer pass safety rules.
problems.evidence: Brenda & Steve had stale weekday/date drift and ungated fee-waiver/no-charge language. Plan was audit-cleaned 2026-04-28 01:40 AM ET (commits bec920d, 0a46a3b); marker at Auto/Client Boxes/Brenda & Steve/2026-04-28_audit_marker.md. The other 27 boxes have not been re-validated.
problems.impact: Future scheduled fires could surface or send unsafe copy if plans are trusted blindly. Violates NS-05 (Safe Movement).
problems.current_workaround: Audit one Client Box at a time. Clean plan/alerts if approved. Leave audit marker. PROB-005 partial fix (verbatim comms backfill) makes auditing easier.
problems.recommended_next_action: Continue with priority queue: Hugo Casillas, Daphney & Frankie, Dawn M Denton, Eliana Lopes, Elizabeth & Peter, Emanuella Andrade, Esther Manu, Flávia Benson, Flaviane Mesquita, then the rest.
close_criteria: all 28 active Client Boxes have been audited
each cleaned plan carries Audit-cleaned: <date> line
each box has YYYY-MM-DD_audit_marker.md
audit findings logged in Audit Ledger with North Star alignment block

problems.title: Approval Flow Does Not Yet Isolate Risky Moves
problems.problem: Risky moves can be approved as part of a batch without enough explicit friction. The system needs a clear approval-card pattern for financial, pricing, scope, enrichment, or high-risk personalization moves.
problems.evidence: Brenda fee-waiver was technically approved by Jake while rushed through batch approval. NS-05 names this as the operative tension: 'smart enough to suggest bold moves, but not autonomous enough to hide risky commitments inside batch approval.'
problems.impact: System can make strategically plausible but authority-sensitive moves without the human fully noticing. Violates NS-05 directly; threatens NS-04.
problems.current_workaround: During plan cleanup, flatten risky future moves or mark them approval-required. Brenda's plan now opens with a Safety Status block requiring guardrails.
problems.recommended_next_action: Design explicit risk approval cards: APPROVE FEE WAIVER, APPROVE ENRICHMENT USE, APPROVE PRICE COMMITMENT, APPROVE SCOPE PROMISE. Risk-card UI not batchable.
close_criteria: approval-card standard exists (component + UX spec)
risk categories defined
approval logged to activity.jsonl
scheduled fires check approval before risky sends
categories mapped to NS-05 risk taxonomy

problems.title: GitHub Direct Writes Can Conflict With Local Unpushed Work
problems.problem: Agents may write directly to GitHub while Jake has local unpushed work.
problems.evidence: After Brenda cleanup commits (bec920d, 0a46a3b), Jake realized local work was not yet pushed. As of 2026-04-28 working tree dirty: M AGENT.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, activity.jsonl, pieces_sweeps.jsonl; ?? LEDGERS/, Flávia Benson.txt.
problems.impact: Merge conflicts, rebase confusion, or accidental overwrites if branch workflow is unclear. Violates NS-09.
problems.current_workaround: This session's pattern: write locally, ledger-log, surface in summary, wait for Jake's go-ahead. Documented in TEMPORAL_CONTINUITY.md §13.
problems.recommended_next_action: Define standard branch workflow: direct-to-main only when Jake confirms tree clean; otherwise feature branch + PR; always report SHAs; never force-push main; coordinate before bundling pre-existing dirty files.
close_criteria: Git workflow policy recorded in Decisions Ledger
Connections Ledger documents branch/push expectations
Communications Ledger entry for 'Jake controls commit/push timing'
AGENT.md / CLAUDE.md updated to reference policy

problems.title: Full Phone Transcripts Not Yet Integrated Into Client Boxes
problems.discovered_by: Jake (recognized the gap during inbox conversation)
problems.problem: Client Boxes had only comms summaries; full phone transcripts and email bodies were missing.
problems.evidence: Jake's 2026-04-28 message about needing full transcripts to ground automation in actual comms.
problems.impact: Plans and customer-facing copy may have over-relied on profile/enrichment because verbatim comms weren't in the box. Violates NS-04 and NS-06.
what_was_fixed: All 28 Client Boxes carry 01b_comms_verbatim.md (chronological with speaker-labeled call transcripts, voicemail transcripts, full email body_text/body_html, SMS, WhatsApp, threads)
comms/<type>_<date>_<id>.json raw payloads, one per activity. 582 raw activity payloads total.
Source-of-truth priority order updated in FILE_DIRECTORY_LEDGER.md §4.3
what_remains_open: Boxes UI page still binds to 01_comms.md only (server.py line 4717)
comms_state_sweep.py does not read verbatim file yet
Plan rewrite trigger after new transcript import not formalized (PROB-006)
Per-box client_ledger.md does not yet reflect verbatim availability
problems.current_workaround: Use existing 01_comms.md plus the new 01b_comms_verbatim.md when reviewing manually. Avoid surfacing facts not clearly in comms.
problems.recommended_next_action: Wire 01b_comms_verbatim.md into BoxesScreen as second comms tab; update comms_state_sweep.py to read verbatim when present; define plan-staleness rule against verbatim file (couples PROB-006).

close_criteria.item: Client Box read protocol updated in automation (not just docs)
Plan rewrite trigger defined when new transcript lands
Boxes UI page surfaces verbatim file

history.note: verbatim comms backfill landed (request_id comms_verbatim_backfill_2026-04-28). Status open → partial. Severity high → medium.

problems.title: Plan Rewrite Trigger After Inbound Needs Formalization
problems.problem: The project agrees conceptually that any meaningful client tap/reply can invalidate the current seven-day plan, but the rewrite trigger and workflow are not formalized in code.
problems.evidence: Jake said any tap should trigger plan re-check. TEMPORAL_CONTINUITY.md §15 documents the rules. client_state_temporal.mmd shows the desired flow. But no field/marker mechanically tracks plan-stale status.
problems.impact: Automation may continue from an old plan after the client's state has changed. Violates NS-05 and 'reply → plan stale' rule.
problems.current_workaround: Manual audits and operator review during scheduled batch approval.
problems.recommended_next_action: Implement state transition in comms_state_sweep.py: new_inbound_detected → active_cadence=paused → plan_status=stale → generate inbound summary → surface for plan rewrite → manual confirmation required.
close_criteria: plan_status field or marker exists in box schema (sibling-schema check first)
comms_state_sweep.py marks plan stale on new-inbound
scheduled fire checks plan freshness
manual confirmation path in UI
documented in Audit Ledger and Decisions Ledger

problems.title: Historical Inbound Detection May Be Noisy
problems.problem: State sweep may mark 'new inbound this cycle' when importing historical inbound activities, not necessarily fresh post-cadence replies.
problems.evidence: Brenda client_ledger.md showed 'New inbound this cycle: True' but listed inbound items from April 19, before cadence start (Day 1 = Monday 2026-04-27). 2026-04-28 verbatim backfill imported additional historical activities — may have re-triggered this in other boxes.
problems.impact: False operator-review alerts; confused plan-pause logic; couples badly with PROB-006 when it lands.
problems.current_workaround: Check timestamps against cadence_started_at before treating inbound as fresh.
problems.recommended_next_action: Audit comms_state_sweep.py inbound detection. Compare against cadence_started_at or last-sweep timestamp. Differentiate 'newly imported historical' from 'new post-cadence inbound.' Update labels.
close_criteria: sweep differentiates newly-imported historical from new post-cadence inbound
client ledger labels are clear (new_post_cadence_inbound: bool vs imported_historical_activities: int)
reply gate uses correct timestamp logic
documented decision in Decisions Ledger

problems.title: Optional Connections Need Verification
problems.problem: Some services are available as connectors or mentioned in environment context, but may not actually be active project dependencies.
problems.evidence: .env lists various credentials; connectors.js and docs/connectors.md exist but haven't been audited against actual import statements / API calls.
problems.impact: Connections Ledger could hallucinate dependencies if not verified.
problems.current_workaround: Mark unverified services as needs-verification when listing.
problems.recommended_next_action: Inspect repo imports, .env.example, server endpoints, and docs/connectors.md. Build Connections Ledger from evidence.
close_criteria: each optional service marked active, optional, or not used based on grep evidence

history.event: closed. Phase 12 Connections Ledger inventoried services with evidence. Single close-criterion fully satisfied.

problems.resolution: CONNECTIONS.md §4 inventories every service with evidence-backed status (active/optional/planned/not-in-use). 11 active + 2 planned + 3 not-in-use, all checked against server.py imports + .env contents + connectors.js + docs/connectors.md.

problems.title: CLAUDE.md Surviving-Domains List Is Out of Date
problems.problem: CLAUDE.md §1 says CCAgentindex/ has 5 surviving domains: people, venues, _ledger, _inbox, indexes. Reality is 26 directories: 10 .gitkeep stubs (Rodbot, commitments, threads, projects, knowledge, tables, annotations, charts) and 16 populated, including heavyweights — intelligence/ (119 files), _inbox/ (77), agent_plans/ (40), people/ (39), venues/ (30), reports/ (28), triggers/ (12).
problems.evidence: Direct inspection on 2026-04-28. Documented in FILE_DIRECTORY_LEDGER.md §4.2.
problems.impact: claude -p subprocesses spawned from /api/delegate read CLAUDE.md as authoritative. They will believe domains like intelligence/, agent_plans/, triggers/, reports/ either don't exist or shouldn't be touched. Violates NS-09 and NS-06.
problems.current_workaround: OPL and FILE_DIRECTORY_LEDGER.md document reality. Agents reading those files first get accurate orientation.
problems.recommended_next_action: Decision needed from Jake: Option A (update CLAUDE.md §1 to match reality), Option B (retire any populated domains that were unintentional), or Option C (add post-trim additions section). Most likely A.
close_criteria: decision recorded in Decisions Ledger
CLAUDE.md §1 surviving-domains list matches actual disk
FILE_DIRECTORY_LEDGER.md §4.2 cross-references updated CLAUDE.md
this problem moves to Recently Closed

problems.title: docs/page_asset_sitemap.md Is Stale
problems.problem: Two page_asset_sitemap.md files exist. Root copy 65 KB updated 2026-04-28 (live UI Done Gate). docs/page_asset_sitemap.md 11 KB abandoned.
problems.evidence: diff confirms different content. Root referenced everywhere; docs/ copy isn't.
problems.impact: An agent could grep for 'sitemap' and edit the wrong file. Violates NS-06.
problems.recommended_next_action: Decision: delete docs/page_asset_sitemap.md or convert to symlink/redirect.
close_criteria: only one page_asset_sitemap.md exists, or any copies are clearly redirect-only
problems.what_fixed_it: Cowork sandbox lacked unlink permission on the mounted user folder; the duplicate was overwritten in place with a redirect stub pointing to the root canonical. Close criterion was deliberately written to accept either delete OR redirect-only.

problems.title: Auto/Boxes/ Mirror Purpose Unclear
problems.problem: Auto/Boxes/ exists as sibling to Auto/orchestrator/ and Auto/comeketo-inbox/, containing mirrors of both with newer mtimes (2026-04-27 16:23 vs 2026-04-26 02:42). _lib.py content was byte-identical.
problems.evidence: Direct inspection. Marked needs-verification in FILE_DIRECTORY_LEDGER.md §7.
problems.impact: Agent could edit wrong copy. Newer mtime is misleading. Possibly abandoned snapshot.
problems.current_workaround: FILE_DIRECTORY_LEDGER.md §10 wrong-turn auto_boxes_as_live warns against editing.
problems.recommended_next_action: Jake to clarify Auto/Boxes/ purpose. If abandoned, delete or archive. If intentional, document and rename.
close_criteria: purpose documented OR directory removed
FDL §7 status changed from needs-verification to active (with role) or removed
FDL §10 wrong-turn updated

problems.title: Two Onboard Scripts/ Folders — Auto/ is canonical, top-level is legacy
problems.discovered_by: Phase 4 FDL inspection (initial misread); corrected by Jake
affected_systems: Onboard Scripts/ (root, 31 files, legacy from old app, being slowly rewritten)
related_files: 7 analytics_*.py at top-level — legacy versions being mined into the analytics page
problems.owner: Jake (informal — gradual migration as analytics work completes)
problems.problem: Initial Phase 4 reading inverted ownership. Top-level Onboard Scripts/ is the legacy from a predecessor app — scripts known to be bad. Auto/Onboard Scripts/ is the legitimate working set translated/reworked from the legacy ones. The 7 analytics_*.py files at top-level are old versions being gradually mined and rewritten into the analytics page.
problems.impact: Low risk now that ownership is corrected. The danger was the inverted assumption.
problems.current_workaround: Treat Auto/Onboard Scripts/ as canonical for new work. Leave top-level alone — source pool being slowly translated. Don't delete; don't sync; don't assume parity.
problems.recommended_next_action: Continue gradual rewrite as analytics page work needs each script. No immediate action.
close_criteria: all needed legacy scripts rewritten/translated
top-level Onboard Scripts/ archived (or marked archive-only)
FDL updated

history.note: created with inverted assumption (top-level treated as canonical superset)
corrected by Jake: Auto/ is canonical/working; top-level is legacy. Severity medium → low. Status needs-decision → triaged. Urgency later → watch.

problems.title: Migrate Auto/ Contents Into CCAgentindex/ → CLOSED via Symlink
problems.resolution: 6 relative symlinks created under CCAgentindex/: Boxes, Client Boxes, comeketo-inbox, Onboard Scripts, orchestrator, Staff Boxes. Each points to ../Auto/<name>. Agent now has bedrock-side access to Auto/ contents as memory without moving anything. Active scheduled fires + seven-day cadence undisturbed. server.py AUTO_ROOT constants keep working. Reversible via unlink.
problems.discovered_by: Jake (migration goal stated during cleanup conversation)
problems.blocked_by: active seven-day setup (Brenda plan + scheduled fires anchored to Day 1 = Monday 2026-04-27); migration would disrupt cadence; revisit after Opus access restored
problems.problem: Everything in Auto/ should eventually live under CCAgentindex/. Auto/ is currently a symlink to /Users/jakeaaron/Desktop/Auto/. Bedrock (CCAgentindex/) is the only filesystem state the app owns, but Client Box truth lives outside it under Auto/.

path_reference_inventory.auto_orchestrator_bin: _lib.py uses relative AUTO = ROOT.parent (would survive move if structure preserved); voice.py has docstring reference

migration_options.label: Sever symlink, copy contents into bedrock
migration_options.summary: Copy Client Boxes/Staff Boxes/orchestrator/comeketo-inbox into new homes under CCAgentindex/. Update server.py constants. Risk: high (live scheduled fires + cadence).

migration_options.label: Symlink CCAgentindex/auto → /Users/jakeaaron/Desktop/Auto/
migration_options.summary: Same content, new access path. Cosmetic; doesn't really solve bedrock-ownership concern.

migration_options.label: Restructure into bedrock domains
migration_options.summary: Each Client Box becomes part of CCAgentindex/people/<slug>/box/. Most aligned with NS-03 but biggest blast radius — touches loader, schemas, render_lead, BoxesScreen.

migration_options.label: Hybrid: keep Auto/ for orchestrator runtime, migrate boxes only
migration_options.summary: Move Auto/Client Boxes/ and Auto/Staff Boxes/ into bedrock; leave Auto/orchestrator/ alone since it's actively running scheduled fires.

problems.current_workaround: Live with the split until Wednesday. Seven-day cadence (Brenda Day 2 of 7 as of 2026-04-28) keeps running undisturbed.
problems.recommended_next_action: Wednesday 2026-04-29 with Opus: pick migration option, plan phased rollout, ensure scheduled fires can be paused safely during cutover, run migration, update server.py constants, verify scheduled fires resume correctly.
close_criteria: migration option selected
phased plan agreed (pause cadence → migrate → verify → resume)
all Auto/ path refs in code updated
scheduled fires + seven-day cadence resume cleanly
FDL §4.1 + §3 updated

problems.title: CCAgentindex/ Bedrock Was Bootstrapped On The Fly — Needs Triad-Based Reconciliation
affected_systems: /api/* endpoints in server.py that read bedrock paths
every Box / Ledger / Sub-agent that has not yet been authored
related_ledgers: North Star NS-03 (single source of truth)
problems.owner: Jake (decision required after ledger + sub-agent buildout settles)
problems.blocked_by: ledger system not yet fully built; sub-agent system not yet fully built; PROB-001, PROB-010
problems.problem: CCAgentindex/ is the bedrock memory substrate the agent reads when running live in the app. It was written on the fly and bootstrapped before the Box+Ledger+Sub-agent triad was the formal philosophy. As a result the bedrock has 32 top-level subdirectories representing categorical thinking from the bootstrap era — many of which should collapse INTO Boxes (per-entity state), Ledgers (legible memory), or Sub-agent packages (operators) once the triad is finished being built out.
problems.evidence: Jake stated 2026-04-28: 'this is essentially the bedrock source of truth memory substrate for our Claude code binary or Codex binary or whatever we're actually running the agent with when we're live inside the application... and this was written on the fly and bootstrapped as I went. Now we actually have our ledger systems being built, our boxes being built, our sub-agents being built, and all of this needs to be aggregated and properly connected and indexed... I just don't think it's a good idea to have like 40 different subdirectories when we're doing it the other way now.'
problems.impact: Two competing organizational models in the same bedrock cause read-time confusion (which directory owns truth?), write-time fragmentation (state about a client could land in 4+ places), loader drift (indexes/index.json keys must change in lockstep with reorganization), and conceptual violation of NS-03 + the legibility-first commitment.
problems.current_workaround: Treat new state-bearing work as triad-shaped (Box+Ledger+Sub-agent). Don't add to commitments/, projects/, threads/, knowledge/ unless an existing sibling already lives there and consistency demands it. Capture migration intent in this entry so it isn't lost.
problems.recommended_next_action: DO NOT ACT YET. Per Jake 2026-04-28: 'the real thing that needs to be done is we need to finish building the ledgers and finish building the sub-agents. Once that's done we can let the dust settle and actually start figuring this all out.' Sequence: (1) finish ledger buildout, (2) finish sub-agent buildout, (3) run reconciliation pass — for each bootstrap-era subdir decide collapse-into-Box / become-Ledger / become-Sub-agent-territory / survive-as-primitive / archive, (4) update indexes/index.json + FILE_DIRECTORY_LEDGER + mission_control_loader.js + CLAUDE.md to match.
close_criteria: Ledger system buildout reached 'active' for all planned ledgers in LEDGERS/INDEX.md
Sub-agent system buildout reached at least one runnable app agent per state-bearing domain
Reconciliation pass completed: each CCAgentindex/ subdirectory has a documented role in the triad model OR has been archived
LEDGERS/FILE_DIRECTORY_LEDGER.md updated to reflect reconciled bedrock
indexes/index.json keys match reconciled bedrock
Project CLAUDE.md describes bedrock organization in triad terms

history.note: created. Jake flagged during catch-up conversation. Deferred pending ledger + sub-agent buildout completion. Inventory of 32 subdirs captured at creation.

recurring_patterns.pattern: Agents may confuse UI/generation surfaces with canonical source files
Plans are written at one moment, then time/client replies/guardrails change. The plan keeps existing as if nothing happened.
recurring_patterns.preventive_approach: plan freshness markers (PROB-006), reply gate, rewrite trigger, audit markers, TEMPORAL §15 plan-aging rules

recurring_patterns.pattern: Docs reflect a project state that has since changed. Agents trust docs and act on stale info.
recurring_patterns.preventive_approach: Done Gate (when DoD lands) + Last Verified dates + cross-references

recurring_patterns.pattern: Same content exists in two places. Edits go to the wrong copy.
recurring_patterns.preventive_approach: FDL §10 Common Wrong-Turns + symlinks where safe + Decisions Ledger to pick canonical

recently_closed.title: Ledger System Not Yet Implemented In Repo
recently_closed.what_fixed_it: Phases 1–4 landed in same session: Global, Temporal Continuity, North Star, File Directory ledgers + INDEX + 9 visuals + 2 Box templates + read-first wiring in CLAUDE.md/AGENT.md/README.md.

verification_evidence.note: Global ↔ Temporal ↔ North Star ↔ File Directory cross-refs

verification_evidence.criterion: Agent read-first workflow points to them
verification_evidence.note: CLAUDE.md §0, AGENT.md Read-second block, README.md Project memory section

recently_closed.successor_work: Phase 5+ ledger buildout — Open Problems (this file), Source-of-Truth, Definition of Done, Decisions, etc. per LEDGERS/INDEX.md build order. Not 'successor problems' — just normal phase work.

local_vs_global_rule: If the problem affects one folder/client only, record locally (e.g. in client_ledger.md or local Box ledger). If it affects a pattern, workflow, source-of-truth rule, safety posture, or multiple systems, record here.
final_operating_rule: A known problem that is not recorded becomes future confusion. If it is broken, name it. If it is risky, classify it. If it is blocked, say why. If it is fixed, prove it.
last_updated_note: Phase 12 — PROB-009 closed (Connections Ledger inventoried services with evidence)
